title: Show HN: Palmier Pro – Open-source macOS video editor built for AI
excerpt: Hi HN, we are Marcos and Harrison, cofounders of Palmier (https://palmier.io). We are building Palmier Pro, an open source macOS video editor, with built-in AI generation and a local MCP server that connects to your agent. Here are a few demos:
content_html:
Hi HN, we are Marcos and Harrison, cofounders of Palmier (https://palmier.io). We are building Palmier Pro, an open source macOS video editor, with built-in AI generation and a local MCP server that connects to your agent. Here are a few demos:

- Making some AI transitions: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hbM_-eR1GX4

- Multicam editing with Codex: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SjS2q2LT1q8

- Cutting long form clips into shorts: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=PR66eN2ouuQ

We built Palmier Pro as an internal tool when we were making AI launch videos for other startups. The main problem it solved in the beginning was the back-and-forth between AI generation platform and video editor. The iteration loop was awkward: AI videos → download → import to editor → edit → realize we need to change the AI video → repeat. So we built a minimal video editor where we could let Claude generate AI videos inside the editor.

As we gave more and more tools to the agent, we wanted to push to see what else agents can do in the video editing space. So today, your Claude/Codex can:

- Manage projects inside Palmier Pro

- Import media from a public URL or filesystem to the project, and organize them in folders

- Search media (by embedding footages using SigLIP2 running locally)

- Edit the timeline (tracks/clips/keyframes operations)

- Generate images, videos, sound effects, captions, music

- Export videos

There are two ways for LLMs to interact with the editor: by connecting to the local MCP server, or using the in-app chat. Both use the same tools and APIs exposed by the video editor.

We have seen people using MCP server to connect to their own workflow to automate massive-scale video editing (e.g. given this same podcast style, replicate it with other footages that I have). We have also seen people using the in-app chat where it lives closer to the editor UI, with lower latency for faster iteration.

We don't believe that AI is going to replace human creativity (nor should it), but where it can really help is in automating the gruntwork in video editing. Most work in the editing workflow is more mechanical than creative. Our vision is to build a video editor where AI can enable more individuals to create.

Throughout our experiments, AI is not very good at creative editing, but given a pattern (transcription-based, beat-based), it can do a decent job at rough cut.

We built Palmier Pro in Swift as an MVP because we wanted better performance and minimal dependencies (no nodejs/webview), with some native macOS APIs like SpeechAnalyzer and CoreML for us to run some models locally. We use SpeechAnalyzer for local transcriber, SigLip2 to embed video frames, beat_this for beat detection, Silero VAD for silence detection, all running locally. The tradeoff is that we don't support Linux or Windows at the moment.

Palmier Pro is open source and free to try out (macOS 26 only at the moment, though - we'll support more platforms over time, but for now are focusing on iterating the core product).

No login required except for the AI generation features, which route requests to our backend. We are offering free credits on sign up so you can try out the AI generation as well.

We'd love to hear your feedback!

blocks.html_en:
Hi HN, we are Marcos and Harrison, cofounders of Palmier (https://palmier.io). We are building Palmier Pro, an open source macOS video editor, with built-in AI generation and a local MCP server that connects to your agent. Here are a few demos:

- Making some AI transitions: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hbM_-eR1GX4

- Multicam editing with Codex: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SjS2q2LT1q8

- Cutting long form clips into shorts: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=PR66eN2ouuQ

We built Palmier Pro as an internal tool when we were making AI launch videos for other startups. The main problem it solved in the beginning was the back-and-forth between AI generation platform and video editor. The iteration loop was awkward: AI videos → download → import to editor → edit → realize we need to change the AI video → repeat. So we built a minimal video editor where we could let Claude generate AI videos inside the editor.

As we gave more and more tools to the agent, we wanted to push to see what else agents can do in the video editing space. So today, your Claude/Codex can:

- Manage projects inside Palmier Pro

- Import media from a public URL or filesystem to the project, and organize them in folders

- Search media (by embedding footages using SigLIP2 running locally)

- Edit the timeline (tracks/clips/keyframes operations)

- Generate images, videos, sound effects, captions, music

- Export videos

There are two ways for LLMs to interact with the editor: by connecting to the local MCP server, or using the in-app chat. Both use the same tools and APIs exposed by the video editor.

We have seen people using MCP server to connect to their own workflow to automate massive-scale video editing (e.g. given this same podcast style, replicate it with other footages that I have). We have also seen people using the in-app chat where it lives closer to the editor UI, with lower latency for faster iteration.

We don't believe that AI is going to replace human creativity (nor should it), but where it can really help is in automating the gruntwork in video editing. Most work in the editing workflow is more mechanical than creative. Our vision is to build a video editor where AI can enable more individuals to create.

Throughout our experiments, AI is not very good at creative editing, but given a pattern (transcription-based, beat-based), it can do a decent job at rough cut.

We built Palmier Pro in Swift as an MVP because we wanted better performance and minimal dependencies (no nodejs/webview), with some native macOS APIs like SpeechAnalyzer and CoreML for us to run some models locally. We use SpeechAnalyzer for local transcriber, SigLip2 to embed video frames, beat_this for beat detection, Silero VAD for silence detection, all running locally. The tradeoff is that we don't support Linux or Windows at the moment.

Palmier Pro is open source and free to try out (macOS 26 only at the moment, though - we'll support more platforms over time, but for now are focusing on iterating the core product).

No login required except for the AI generation features, which route requests to our backend. We are offering free credits on sign up so you can try out the AI generation as well.

We'd love to hear your feedback!

sentences.en: Hi HN, we are Marcos and Harrison, cofounders of Palmier (https://palmier.io). We are building Palmier Pro, an open source macOS video editor, with built-in AI generation and a local MCP server that connects to your agent.
sentences.zh: 您好，HN，我们是 Palmier (https://palmier.io) 的联合创始人 Marcos 和 Harrison。我们正在构建 Palmier Pro，这是一款开源 macOS 视频编辑器，具有内置 AI 生成功能和连接到您的代理的本地 MCP 服务器。

sentences.en: Here are a few demos:- Making some AI transitions: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hbM_-eR1GX4- Multicam editing with Codex: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SjS2q2LT1q8- Cutting long form clips into shorts: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=PR66eN2ouuQWe built Palmier Pro as an internal tool when we were making AI launch videos for other startups.
sentences.zh: 以下是一些演示：- 进行一些 AI 过渡：https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hbM_-eR1GX4- 使用 Codex 进行多机位编辑：https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SjS2q2LT1q8- 将长格式剪辑剪成短片：https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=PR66eN2ouuQ当我们制作 AI 启动视频时，我们构建了 Palmier Pro 作为内部工具其他初创公司。

sentences.en: The main problem it solved in the beginning was the back-and-forth between AI generation platform and video editor.
sentences.zh: 它一开始主要解决的问题是AI生成平台和视频编辑器之间的来回切换。

sentences.en: The iteration loop was awkward: AI videos → download → import to editor → edit → realize we need to change the AI video → repeat.
sentences.zh: 迭代循环很尴尬：AI视频→下载→导入编辑器→编辑→意识到我们需要更改AI视频→重复。

sentences.en: So we built a minimal video editor where we could let Claude generate AI videos inside the editor.As we gave more and more tools to the agent, we wanted to push to see what else agents can do in the video editing space.
sentences.zh: 因此，我们构建了一个最小的视频编辑器，让 Claude 在编辑器中生成 AI 视频。随着我们为代理提供越来越多的工具，我们希望推动看看代理在视频编辑领域还能做什么。

sentences.en: So today, your Claude/Codex can:- Manage projects inside Palmier Pro- Import media from a public URL or filesystem to the project, and organize them in folders- Search media (by embedding footages using SigLIP2 running locally)- Edit the timeline (tracks/clips/keyframes operations)- Generate images, videos, sound effects, captions, music- Export videosThere are two ways for LLMs to interact with the editor: by connecting to the local MCP server, or using the in-app chat.
sentences.zh: 因此，今天，您的 Claude/Codex 可以： - 在 Palmier Pro 中管理项目 - 将媒体从公共 URL 或文件系统导入到项目中，并将它们组织到文件夹中 - 搜索媒体（通过使用本地运行的 SigLIP2 嵌入素材） - 编辑时间线（轨道/剪辑/关键帧操作） - 生成图像、视频、音效、字幕、音乐 - 导出视频 LLM 有两种与编辑器交互的方式：连接到本地 MCP 服务器，或使用应用内聊天。

sentences.en: Both use the same tools and APIs exposed by the video editor.We have seen people using MCP server to connect to their own workflow to automate massive-scale video editing (e.g. given this same podcast style, replicate it with other footages that I have).
sentences.zh: 两者都使用视频编辑器公开的相同工具和 API。我们已经看到人们使用 MCP 服务器连接到他们自己的工作流程来自动化大规模视频编辑（例如，给定相同的播客风格，将其与我拥有的其他素材复制）。

sentences.en: We have also seen people using the in-app chat where it lives closer to the editor UI, with lower latency for faster iteration.We don't believe that AI is going to replace human creativity (nor should it), but where it can really help is in automating the gruntwork in video editing.
sentences.zh: 我们还看到人们使用应用内聊天，它更接近编辑器 UI，延迟更低，迭代速度更快。我们不相信人工智能会取代人类的创造力（也不应该取代），但它真正能提供帮助的是自动化视频编辑中的繁重工作。

sentences.en: Most work in the editing workflow is more mechanical than creative.
sentences.zh: 编辑工作流程中的大多数工作机械性多于创造性。

sentences.en: Our vision is to build a video editor where AI can enable more individuals to create.Throughout our experiments, AI is not very good at creative editing, but given a pattern (transcription-based, beat-based), it can do a decent job at rough cut.We built Palmier Pro in Swift as an MVP because we wanted better performance and minimal dependencies (no nodejs/webview), with some native macOS APIs like SpeechAnalyzer and CoreML for us to run some models locally.
sentences.zh: 我们的愿景是建立一个视频编辑器，人工智能可以让更多的人进行创作。在我们的实验中，人工智能不太擅长创意编辑，但给定一种模式（基于转录、基于节拍），它可以在粗剪方面做得不错。我们在 Swift 中构建 Palmier Pro 作为 MVP，因为我们想要更好的性能和最小的依赖关系（没有 Nodejs/WebView），并使用一些原生 macOS API（如 SpeechAnalyzer 和 CoreML）供我们在本地运行一些模型。

sentences.en: We use SpeechAnalyzer for local transcriber, SigLip2 to embed video frames, beat_this for beat detection, Silero VAD for silence detection, all running locally.
sentences.zh: 我们使用 SpeechAnalyzer 进行本地转录器，使用 SigLip2 嵌入视频帧，使用beat_this进行节拍检测，使用 Silero VAD 进行静音检测，所有这些都在本地运行。

sentences.en: The tradeoff is that we don't support Linux or Windows at the moment.Palmier Pro is open source and free to try out (macOS 26 only at the moment, though - we'll support more platforms over time, but for now are focusing on iterating the core product).No login required except for the AI generation features, which route requests to our backend.
sentences.zh: 权衡是我们目前不支持 Linux 或 Windows。Palmier Pro 是开源的，可以免费试用（不过目前仅支持 macOS 26 - 随着时间的推移，我们将支持更多平台，但目前正专注于迭代核心产品）。除了 AI 生成功能（将请求路由到我们的后端）之外，无需登录。

sentences.en: We are offering free credits on sign up so you can try out the AI generation as well.We'd love to hear your feedback!
sentences.zh: 我们在注册时提供免费积分，以便您也可以尝试人工智能生成。我们很乐意听到您的反馈！

sentences.en: We are offering free credits on sign up so you can try out the AI generation as well.
sentences.zh: 我们在注册时提供免费积分，因此您也可以尝试人工智能生成。

blocks.html_en: We'd love to hear your feedback!

sentences.en: We'd love to hear your feedback!
sentences.zh: 我们很想听听您的反馈！

content_html_zh: 您好，HN，我们是 Palmier (https://palmier.io) 的联合创始人 Marcos 和 Harrison。我们正在构建 Palmier Pro，这是一款开源 macOS 视频编辑器，具有内置 AI 生成功能和连接到您的代理的本地 MCP 服务器。 以下是一些演示：- 进行一些 AI 过渡：https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hbM_-eR1GX4- 使用 Codex 进行多机位编辑：https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SjS2q2LT1q8- 将长格式剪辑剪成短片：https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=PR66eN2ouuQ当我们制作 AI 启动视频时，我们构建了 Palmier Pro 作为内部工具其他初创公司。 它一开始主要解决的问题是AI生成平台和视频编辑器之间的来回切换。 迭代循环很尴尬：AI视频→下载→导入编辑器→编辑→意识到我们需要更改AI视频→重复。 因此，我们构建了一个最小的视频编辑器，让 Claude 在编辑器中生成 AI 视频。随着我们为代理提供越来越多的工具，我们希望推动看看代理在视频编辑领域还能做什么。 因此，今天，您的 Claude/Codex 可以： - 在 Palmier Pro 中管理项目 - 将媒体从公共 URL 或文件系统导入到项目中，并将它们组织到文件夹中 - 搜索媒体（通过使用本地运行的 SigLIP2 嵌入素材） - 编辑时间线（轨道/剪辑/关键帧操作） - 生成图像、视频、音效、字幕、音乐 - 导出视频 LLM 有两种与编辑器交互的方式：连接到本地 MCP 服务器，或使用应用内聊天。 两者都使用视频编辑器公开的相同工具和 API。我们已经看到人们使用 MCP 服务器连接到他们自己的工作流程来自动化大规模视频编辑（例如，给定相同的播客风格，将其与我拥有的其他素材复制）。 我们还看到人们使用应用内聊天，它更接近编辑器 UI，延迟更低，迭代速度更快。我们不相信人工智能会取代人类的创造力（也不应该取代），但它真正能提供帮助的是自动化视频编辑中的繁重工作。 编辑工作流程中的大多数工作机械性多于创造性。 我们的愿景是建立一个视频编辑器，人工智能可以让更多的人进行创作。在我们的实验中，人工智能不太擅长创意编辑，但给定一种模式（基于转录、基于节拍），它可以在粗剪方面做得不错。我们在 Swift 中构建 Palmier Pro 作为 MVP，因为我们想要更好的性能和最小的依赖关系（没有 Nodejs/WebView），并使用一些原生 macOS API（如 SpeechAnalyzer 和 CoreML）供我们在本地运行一些模型。 我们使用 SpeechAnalyzer 进行本地转录器，使用 SigLip2 嵌入视频帧，使用beat_this进行节拍检测，使用 Silero VAD 进行静音检测，所有这些都在本地运行。 权衡是我们目前不支持 Linux 或 Windows。Palmier Pro 是开源的，可以免费试用（不过目前仅支持 macOS 26 - 随着时间的推移，我们将支持更多平台，但目前正专注于迭代核心产品）。除了 AI 生成功能（将请求路由到我们的后端）之外，无需登录。 我们在注册时提供免费积分，以便您也可以尝试人工智能生成。我们很乐意听到您的反馈！
